summary: Calibrate statistical framework for realistic scale using MDES instead of required-N recommendations
context: Power analysis was producing unhelpful "need 500 runs" when runs cost ~$100 each. Real data: 7 scenarios × 8 conditions at 3 iterations = n=21/condition (MDES d≥0.80), at 5 iterations = n=35/condition (MDES d≥0.62). Observed effects were d=0.15-0.42, below detection threshold.
rationale: Added minimum_detectable_effect(n) to stats.py. Reframed reliability.py around MDES with design_guidance section. Changed recommendations from alarming "need N runs" to actionable "at your n, only effects ≥X are detectable." Marked effect sizes below MDES as inconclusive in terminal/report output. This gives researchers honest, actionable guidance instead of impossible sample size targets.

alternatives.option: Keep required-N recommendations as-is with documentation caveats
alternatives.reason_rejected: Telling users they need 500 runs at $100/run is actively unhelpful and erodes trust in the analysis tool. MDES reframing provides the same statistical information in actionable form.
